result.claim: The raw dataset contains 1,282 samples collected from 126 unique scientific articles.
result.original_text: We manually collected 1282 raw samples from 126 unique scientific articles (Table S1, Supporting Information).

result.claim: After cleaning, the final dataset includes more than 980 nanoparticle entries.
result.original_text: After minimal data cleansing, we obtained the dataset of more than 980 nanosized metals, bimetals, and metal oxides ...

result.claim: The data were split into training and test sets using an 80/20 ratio.
result.original_text: We normalized and log‑scaled the data for each problem before splitting to train and test sets in the 80/20 ratio.

result.claim: Four specific tree‑based models were evaluated: Random Forest, Extra Trees, LGBMRegressor, and XGBRegressor.
result.original_text: Random Forest and Extra Trees regression models ... LGBMRegressor and XGBRegressor algorithms build sequences of decision trees.
result.value: Random Forest, Extra Trees, LGBMRegressor, XGBRegressor

result.claim: Hyperparameter optimization was performed with the Optuna framework.
result.original_text: hyperparameter tuning was done using the model‑agnostic Optuna framework (Table S5, Supporting Information).

result.claim: Ten‑fold cross‑validation was used for model evaluation.
The SAR prediction model achieved an R² of 0.86 on test data.
result.original_text: Model‑agnostic hyperparameter optimization yielded state‑of‑the‑art performance of R² = 0.86 ... for prediction of SAR.

result.claim: The r1 relaxivity model achieved an R² of 0.78 on test data.
result.original_text: R² = 0.78 ... for prediction of r1 relaxivity.

result.claim: The r2 relaxivity model achieved an R² of 0.75 on test data.
result.original_text: R² = 0.75 ... for prediction of r2 relaxivity.

result.claim: RMSE for SAR prediction decreased from 0.28 (cross‑validation) to 0.26 (test set).
result.original_text: For SAR prediction, RMSE of 0.28 on tenfold cross‑validation dropped to 0.26 on test samples.

result.claim: The SAR, r1, and r2 regression tasks used 460, 355, and 465 samples respectively.
result.original_text: prediction of SAR value, r1 or r2 relaxivity containing 460, 355, and 465 samples, respectively.

result.claim: k‑Nearest Neighbors imputation was applied to fill missing feature values.
result.original_text: For other parameters ... we chose kNN imputation technique, because it considers the relationships between the different features.

result.claim: Features with >50% missing values (zeta potential, hydrodynamic radius) were excluded from modeling.
result.original_text: zeta potential and hydrodynamic radius ... had to be excluded due to the large percent of missing values (>50%).

result.claim: Highly correlated geometrical features were reduced to area‑to‑volume and max‑to‑min length ratios.
result.original_text: We transformed highly correlated geometrical features into area to volume and max to min length ratios reflecting size and shape anisotropy.
